name: Haywood v. Drown
question: Does a state law barring civil claims against corrections officers violate the Supremacy Clause of the United States Constitution when it prohibits an inmate from bringing a claim for a violation of his civil rights under 42 U.S.C. 1983?
description: A case in which the Court found that prohibiting civil claims against correction officers in their official capacities violated the Supremacy Clause of the Constitution.
